parsed.summary: The v0.4.6 diff introduces retry/backoff and error-dispatch infrastructure (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, glm-client.mjs) that ships with good test coverage but carries material risk: the retry loop has a subtle correctness issue around jitter math that can produce zero-delay retries on re-entrancy, and the GLM client's error-classification path can silently swallow non-classifiable errors by converting them to generic Error instances, discarding the original HTTP status and response body. These are exactly the kind of silent-degradation failures that are expensive to diagnose in production.

findings.title: Retry jitter can produce zero delay when base delay rounds to zero
findings.body: In retry.mjs, the jitter calculation `Math.floor(Math.random() * delay)` where delay is computed as `baseDelay * 2 ** attempt` can produce a jitter value of 0, and the resulting delay can also be 0 when baseDelay is small and attempt is 0. This means the first retry fires immediately with no backoff. Under sustained failures this collapses into a tight retry loop that can exhaust rate limits or amplify load on an already-degraded dependency. The tests only check that delay is within bounds, not that it is strictly positive.
findings.recommendation: Add a floor to the computed delay, e.g. `const jitteredDelay = Math.max(1, delay - jitter)` or `delay = Math.max(minDelay, baseDelay * 2 ** attempt)`. Add an explicit test case that asserts delay > 0 for all attempts up to maxRetries.

findings.title: GLM client error wrapping can discard original HTTP status and response body for unclassified errors
findings.body: In glm-client.mjs, when BigModel error classification does not match a known pattern, the code creates a generic Error, losing the original status code and response body. Callers upstream cannot distinguish a 500 (retryable) from a 403 (not retryable) because the classification layer consumed and discarded that information. This creates a silent failure path where non-retryable auth/permission errors get retried until maxRetries, wasting quota and delaying user-visible errors.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the fallback Error path preserves `status`, `headers`, and a truncated `body` property on the error object. Consider adding an `originalError` or `cause` reference so that debugging production failures does not require guessing what BigModel returned.

findings.title: pre-push hook runs release-readiness check that can block legitimate pushes without override
findings.body: The pre-push hook at scripts/hooks/pre-push invokes check-release-ready.sh, which can fail and block `git push`. There is no documented `--no-verify` escape hatch or skip mechanism in the hook itself. If a developer needs to push a WIP branch or a hotfix that intentionally skips version bumps, the hook will reject the push. This is a developer-experience footgun that can delay critical patches.
findings.recommendation: Document the `--no-verify` git flag in a comment inside the hook or in README. Alternatively, make the check conditional on the branch name (e.g. only enforce on main/develop) so feature branches are not blocked.

next_steps: Inspect retry.mjs jitter math and add a positive floor guard with a test
Audit glm-client.mjs error fallback path to ensure HTTP status/body are preserved on the thrown error
Verify that bigmodel-errors.mjs classification covers all documented BigModel error codes, not just a subset
Confirm pre-push hook behavior on non-release branches and document skip mechanism
